title: Permanent residence fees increasing on April 30, 2024 - Canada.ca
content:
## Permanent residence fees increasing on April 30 , 2024
Ottawa, April 11, 2024 – On April 30, 2024, fees for all permanent residence applications are increasing as part of a routine update. These fees increase every 2 years to manage growing program and service delivery costs and to keep up with inflation.
If you plan on submitting a permanent resident application on or after April 30, consult the chart below, which lists the impacted programs and fee increases.
New fee as of April 30, 2024
Right of permanent residence fee
Principal applicant and accompanying spouse or common-law partner
Provincial Nominee Program; federal skilled worker class, Quebec skilled worker class; Atlantic Immigration class; and most economic immigration pilots (rural, agri-food)
Accompanying spouse or common-law partner
Accompanying dependent child
Live-in Caregiver Program and caregivers pilots (Home Child Care Provider Pilot and Home Support Worker Pilot)
Accompanying spouse or common-law partner
Accompanying dependent child
Business (federal and Quebec)
Accompanying spouse or common-law partner
Accompanying dependent child
Family reunification (spouses or common-law partners and children; parents and grandparents; and other relatives)
Sponsored principal applicant
Sponsored dependent child
Accompanying spouse or common-law partner
Accompanying dependent child
Accompanying spouse or common-law partner
Accompanying dependent child
Humanitarian and compassionate or public policy
Accompanying spouse or common-law partner
Accompanying dependent child
